skill.name: agentdb-vector-search-optimization
skill.description: Optimize AgentDB vector search with benchmarking, quantization, HNSW indexing, caching, and batch operations for large vector datasets.
skill.summary: Tune AgentDB vector search for lower memory use, faster queries, and measurable benchmark results.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command, network URL, and weak cryptography patterns. Review found most backtick and weak-crypto alerts are false positives from Markdown code fences and numeric optimization claims, but the README does instruct users to install and run external Node tooling.

medium_findings.title: External Package Execution in Quick Start
medium_findings.description: README.md instructs users to install an external npm package and run a ts-node script. This is a legitimate setup pattern, but it carries supply chain and local code execution risk if users run it without reviewing the package and script.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly present in a bash quick start block. The intent appears operational rather than malicious, so the concern is execution and supply chain exposure rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence Backticks Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: PROCESS.md and SKILL.md contain Markdown code fences and TypeScript examples for benchmarking, quantization, indexing, and caching. These are documentation examples, not Ruby or shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown fences or fenced TypeScript snippets. No evidence of Ruby backtick execution or shell interpolation was found at these locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Is Not Supported by Evidence
low_findings.description: The high-severity static alert points to SKILL.md line 4, which is a natural language description of optimization ratios and HNSW indexing. No cryptographic primitive or weak algorithm use is present there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 4 contains descriptive metadata about memory reduction and search performance. I found no cryptographic algorithm call or crypto implementation evidence at that line.

low_findings.title: Documentation URL Is Benign
low_findings.description: SKILL.md links to AgentDB optimization documentation. This creates an external navigation point, but no data transmission, credential handling, or hidden network behavior is shown.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is a plain documentation URL in an Additional Resources section. It does not perform a request or include sensitive data.

dangerous_patterns.title: Install and Run External Node Tooling
dangerous_patterns.description: The quick start asks users to install agentdb-optimization and run optimize-db.ts through npx ts-node. Users should review package provenance and local scripts before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and executable. The content does not show malicious intent, but the pattern can execute untrusted code in a user's environment.

content.value_statement: Large vector stores can become slow, costly, and hard to benchmark. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through AgentDB profiling, quantization, indexing, caching, and validation.
actual_capabilities: Defines a five-phase workflow for measuring and improving AgentDB vector search performance.
Explains quantization options, including product, scalar, and binary quantization.
Guides HNSW index configuration with parameters such as M, efConstruction, and efSearch.
Covers query, result, and embedding cache strategies with hit rate targets.
Provides benchmark targets for memory use, latency, throughput, and accuracy.
Identifies supporting agent roles for performance, machine learning, and backend implementation tasks.
limitations: Does not include a complete production implementation of AgentDB optimization code.
Requires users to understand AgentDB basics and vector search concepts.
Performance claims depend on dataset size, embeddings, hardware, and workload shape.
External package commands should be reviewed before running in a local environment.

use_cases.title: Reduce Search Latency
use_cases.description: Tune indexing and cache settings to lower p95 search latency for an existing AgentDB deployment.

use_cases.title: Lower Vector Storage Memory
use_cases.description: Evaluate quantization choices that reduce memory use while preserving acceptable retrieval accuracy.

use_cases.title: Plan Scale Testing
use_cases.description: Create a benchmark plan for millions of vectors with clear success metrics and comparison steps.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Baseline Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to help me measure baseline AgentDB vector search latency, throughput, memory use, and accuracy.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for an existing vector database

prompt_templates.title: Choose Quantization Settings
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare product, scalar, and binary quantization for my AgentDB dataset. Recommend settings that balance memory reduction and accuracy.

prompt_templates.title: Tune HNSW and Cache
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me tune HNSW parameters and query cache settings for a high-throughput AgentDB workload with strict latency goals.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Validation Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a before-and-after validation plan for AgentDB optimization, including metrics, test phases, acceptance thresholds, and rollback checks.

output_examples.input: I need to optimize AgentDB for a growing semantic search workload.
output_examples.output: A staged plan that measures current latency, applies quantization, builds an HNSW index, configures caching, and validates results.

output_examples.input: My vector store uses too much memory after adding more embeddings.
output_examples.output: A comparison of quantization approaches with expected tradeoffs for compression, recall, training cost, and operational complexity.

output_examples.input: We need proof that optimization did not reduce search quality.
output_examples.output: A benchmark checklist covering recall, p95 latency, throughput, memory reduction, cache hit rate, and before-and-after comparisons.

best_practices: Measure baseline latency, throughput, memory, and accuracy before changing configuration.
Validate recall after each quantization or indexing change.
Review external packages and scripts before running quick-start commands.
anti_patterns: Do not apply maximum compression without measuring accuracy loss.
Do not tune HNSW parameters only against a small sample workload.
Do not treat published speedup claims as guaranteed production results.

faq.question: What does this skill optimize?
faq.answer: It focuses on AgentDB vector search memory use, query latency, throughput, caching, and benchmark validation.

faq.question: Does it require AgentDB experience?
faq.answer: Yes. Users should understand basic AgentDB operations and vector search concepts before applying the workflow.

faq.question: Can it guarantee a 150x speedup?
faq.answer: No. The documented target depends on data size, index settings, hardware, workload, and accuracy requirements.

faq.question: Does it include runnable production code?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance and example snippets, not a complete production implementation.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The quick start includes npm and npx commands. Users should review external packages and scripts before execution.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
